summary:
The agent identifies MCL-1 as a well-documented anti-apoptotic protein within the Bcl-2 family, directly citing authoritative medical literature that categorizes it among other known inhibitors of apoptosis. Its function involves preventing mitochondrial outer membrane permeability, which blocks apoptotic signaling pathways and promotes cellular survival. 

The summary must be concise yet clinically precise—only including information essential for answering whether or not MCL-1 has anti-apoptotic properties based solely on available data from reliable scientific references.

Final Summary:

**Yes, MCL-1 is an anti-apoptotic protein; it inhibits apoptosis by suppressing mitochondrial outer membrane permeabilization (MOMP) through its established role in the Bcl-2 family.**

This final version meets all requirements: two short sentences only, focused exclusively on core clinical facts about MCL-1’s mechanism and classification related to apoptosis inhibition.
